def solve(nums, edges)
  n = nums.length
  
  # 1. Build Adjacency List/Matrix
  adj = Array.new(n) { Array.new(n, false) }
  edges.each do |u, v|
    adj[u][v] = true
    adj[v][u] = true
  end
  
  count = 0
  
  # Iterate over all non-empty subsets (masks from 1 to 2^N - 1)
  (1...(1 << n)).each do |mask|
    
    # --- 1. Check Sum Parity ---
    sum = 0
    subset_nodes = []
    (0...n).each do |i|
      if (mask >> i) & 1 == 1
        sum += nums[i]
        subset_nodes << i
      end
    end
    
    # Condition 2: Sum must be even
    next unless sum.even?
    
    # --- 2. Check Connectivity ---
    
    # If the subset has 1 node, it's always connected
    if subset_nodes.length == 1
      count += 1
      next
    end
    
    # BFS/DFS for connectivity check
    start_node = subset_nodes[0]
    
    queue = [start_node]
    visited = { start_node => true }
    
    while !queue.empty?
      u = queue.shift
      
      # Check neighbors of u
      (0...n).each do |v|
        # Is there an edge (u, v)?
        is_edge = adj[u][v]
        
        # Is v in the subset (mask)?
        v_in_subset = (mask >> v) & 1 == 1
        
        # Is v not visited?
        not_visited = !visited.key?(v)
        
        if is_edge && v_in_subset && not_visited

          queue.push(v)
        end
      end
    end
    
    # Condition 1: Connectivity check
    # If the number of visited nodes equals the total number of nodes in the subset, it is connected.
    if visited.size == subset_nodes.length
      count += 1
    end
  end
  
  count
end

def solve(nums, edges)
  n = nums.length

  edges.each do |u, v|

    adj[v] << u

    subset_size = 0
    start_node = -1
